name: Hexapawn
author: Martin Gardner (1962) / AGP port
description: Hexapawn (Martin Gardner, 1962) on a 3x3 board — three pawns per side. Pawns move like chess pawns: push one square straight forward to an empty square, or capture one square diagonally forward. No double-step, en passant or promotion. You win by reaching the far rank or by leaving your opponent with no move. Famous as a matchbox machine-learning teaching game; with perfect play the second player wins.
